mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Grafana instances and their resources in Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who prefer managing their observability stack using GitOps workflows or infrastructure as code. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes CRDs and potential complexity in multi-instance setups.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command sets up the repository for future installations.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator in your Kubernetes cluster using the specified version.

```bash

helm install grafana-operator grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> This installs the Grafana Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource (CR) for Grafana, defining its configuration and settings.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: secret
EOF

```

> This command creates a Grafana instance with basic configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: secret
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> This outputs the details of the Grafana instance, including its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated Grafana instances and their configurations, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> This command uninstalls the Grafana Operator.
> This command deletes the Grafana instance created earlier.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Grafana configurations. This process involves upgrading the Helm release and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Backup the current Grafana configuration.
> This command upgrades the Grafana Operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the Grafana instance is running correctly after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Grafana" in version "grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the Grafana CRD is not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Grafana Operator is installed correctly and the CRDs are applied.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install Grafana: chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Helm chart repository is not added or updated.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo add grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts' and 'helm repo update'.

troubleshooting.title: Grafana instance is not starting.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to misconfiguration in the Grafana CR.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Grafana pod using 'kubectl logs <grafana-pod-name>' for detailed error messages.

troubleshooting.title: Dashboard not loading in Grafana.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to incorrect JSON configuration in the GrafanaDashboard CR.

**Fix:**
Verify the JSON structure in the GrafanaDashboard CR and correct any syntax errors.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features and improved CRD validation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get grafana'.

    security:

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
